phase_note: Phase 1 inventories expression precedents and reserves deeper operator, closure, and interpolation stress tests for follow-up slices.

cases.upstream_focus: Function-call expressions exercise argument evaluation shape, arity behavior, and nested expression execution.
cases.zr_decision: ZR expression tests should expand from simple arithmetic into nested call expressions with observable ordering and arity-sensitive behavior.

cases.upstream_focus: Closures validate expression capture, nested scope lookup, and delayed execution semantics.
cases.zr_decision: ZR expression coverage must include nested closures and captured locals, not only top-level immediate expressions.

cases.upstream_focus: Operator precedence, conditional evaluation, and member-access expressions need an executable regression baseline.
cases.zr_decision: ZR should validate precedence and member-chain evaluation using runnable fixtures instead of parser-only samples.

cases.upstream_focus: Grammar-level expression coverage needs breadth across nesting forms and boundary syntax, not just one or two happy paths.
cases.zr_decision: ZR expression manifests will drive broader parser fixtures for nesting, precedence, and mixed expression forms.

cases.upstream_focus: Embedded expression regions stress parse boundaries where literal and expression grammars interleave.
cases.zr_decision: ZR will keep interpolation-like or embedded-expression work separate from plain string parsing but inside the same expression inventory.

cases.upstream_focus: Expression parsing at macro-like boundaries needs explicit negative tests for bad argument forms and recovery.
cases.zr_decision: ZR should add future negative expression fixtures that assert clean failure when expression lists and named arguments are malformed.
